title: Pellionia repens
taxonomy: Pellionia repens is classified under the family Urticaceae. This plant is a member of the order Urticales, which consists of various families of flowering plants. Pellionia repens is also classified as a species within the genus Pellionia. The classification of Pellionia repens is further supported by its unique characteristics and features, such as its habit of forming dense mats and its climbing habit. Further research may be necessary for a complete understanding of the classification of Pellionia repens.
uses: Pellionia repens is used in traditional medicine in Southeast Asia, particularly in Malaysia and Thailand. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In folk medicine, it is used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The leaves and stems of Pellionia repens are often consumed as a tea or applied topically. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and potential side effects of using Pellionia repens for medicinal purposes.

It is said to help in reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation, and improving overall well-being. side_effects: Pellionia repens is known for its potential side effects when ingested, which may include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea.
